content.text: Management Subscription

The Management subscription is a dedicated Azure subscription inside the Enterprise-Scale Landing Zone (ESLZ) platform layer. Its single purpose is to host the centralised observability and operations tooling that every other subscription in the hierarchy depends on: a Log Analytics workspace that aggregates diagnostic data from all spokes, an Automation Account for update and configuration management, and the Azure Monitor alert rules that page on-call engineers when something goes wrong. Keeping these resources in their own subscription enforces a clean blast radius — a misconfigured workload landing zone cannot delete, modify, or exhaust the quotas of the monitoring infrastructure.

attrs.alt: ESLZ Management Subscription architecture diagram showing Log Analytics workspace and Automation Account receiving diagnostic logs from spoke subscriptions

content.text: Platform rule: every resource in every landing zone subscription must emit diagnostic logs and metrics to the central Log Analytics workspace. Azure Policy (DeployIfNotExists) enforces this automatically — no workload team has to remember to wire it up.
Management Subscription Contents
The subscription contains a single resource group. All platform management resources are co-located there so that RBAC and cost tagging can be applied uniformly.
Resource
Name Pattern
Purpose
Resource Group
rg-management-<env>-<region>
Container for all management resources
Log Analytics Workspace
law-mgmt-<env>-<region>
Central log and metrics aggregation
Automation Account
aa-mgmt-<env>-<region>
Update Management, DSC, runbooks
Data Collection Rules
dcr-<source>-<env>
Route specific log streams to workspace tables
Action Groups
ag-<severity>-<env>
Email / webhook / ITSM notification targets
Alert Rules
alert-<resource>-<condition>
Metric, log, and activity-log alerts
Microsoft Sentinel
(optional) sentinel-<env>
SIEM / SOAR built on top of the workspace
Recovery Services Vault
rsv-mgmt-<env>-<region>
Platform VM backup (optional, DR tier)
Deploying the Management Baseline

The commands below provision the core infrastructure. Run them once per environment (prod, nonprod) from a principal that holds Owner on the Management subscription. The Automation Account is linked to the workspace during creation so that the Update Management solution can query patch compliance data directly from Log Analytics.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Variables — adjust to match your naming convention
SUB_ID="<management-subscription-id>"
RG="rg-management-prod-eastus"
LOCATION="eastus"
LAW="law-mgmt-prod-eastus"
AA="aa-mgmt-prod-eastus"

az account set --subscription "$SUB_ID"

# 1. Resource group
az group create \
  --name "$RG" \
  --location "$LOCATION" \
  --tags environment=prod platform=management

# 2. Log Analytics workspace (PerGB2018 pricing, 90-day retention)
az monitor log-analytics workspace create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --workspace-name "$LAW" \
  --location "$LOCATION" \
  --sku PerGB2018 \
  --retention-time 90

LAW_ID=$(az monitor log-analytics workspace show \
  --resource-group "$RG" --workspace-name "$LAW" \
  --query id --output tsv)

# 3. Automation Account (same region — required for workspace link)
az automation account create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --automation-account-name "$AA" \
  --location "$LOCATION" \
  --sku Basic

AA_ID=$(az automation account show \
  --resource-group "$RG" --name "$AA" \
  --query id --output tsv)

# 4. Link Automation Account to Log Analytics workspace
#    (enables Update Management, Change Tracking, Inventory solutions)
az rest --method PUT \
  --uri "${LAW_ID}/linkedServices/Automation?api-version=2020-08-01" \
  --body "{\"properties\": {\"resourceId\": \"${AA_ID}\"}}"

echo "Management baseline deployed."
Configuring Azure Monitor Alerts

Platform alerts live in the Management subscription but target resources across all subscriptions via scoped alert rules. An Action Group defines where notifications go; alert rules reference the group by resource ID so that changing a recipient address requires only one update.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
RG="rg-management-prod-eastus"
LAW="law-mgmt-prod-eastus"
AG="ag-critical-prod"

LAW_ID=$(az monitor log-analytics workspace show \
  --resource-group "$RG" --workspace-name "$LAW" \
  --query id --output tsv)

# 1. Action group — email + webhook
az monitor action-group create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --name "$AG" \
  --short-name "CritProd" \
  --action email ops-team ops@example.com \
  --action webhook pagerduty https://events.pagerduty.com/integration/<key>/enqueue

AG_ID=$(az monitor action-group show \
  --resource-group "$RG" --name "$AG" \
  --query id --output tsv)

# 2. Activity log alert — Azure service health events (subscription scope)
az monitor activity-log alert create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --name "alert-service-health-prod" \
  --scopes "/subscriptions/<management-subscription-id>" \
  --condition "category=ServiceHealth" \
  --action-group "$AG_ID" \
  --description "Fires on any Azure service health incident affecting this subscription"

# 3. Metric alert — VM CPU > 90 % for 5 minutes (platform VMs)
az monitor metrics alert create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --name "alert-vm-cpu-high-prod" \
  --scopes "/subscriptions/<management-subscription-id>/resourceGroups/$RG" \
  --condition "avg Percentage CPU > 90" \
  --window-size 5m \
  --evaluation-frequency 1m \
  --action "$AG_ID" \
  --description "Platform VM CPU sustained above 90 % for 5 minutes"

echo "Alert rules configured."
Update Management with Azure Automation

Azure Automation Update Management uses the linked Log Analytics workspace to inventory installed patches and track compliance across all onboarded machines — including VMs in spoke subscriptions once they have the MMA or Azure Monitor Agent installed. A maintenance window (schedule) controls when updates are applied so that patching never occurs during business hours.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
RG="rg-management-prod-eastus"
AA="aa-mgmt-prod-eastus"
LAW="law-mgmt-prod-eastus"
SCHEDULE="sched-patch-sunday-2am"

LAW_WORKSPACE_ID=$(az monitor log-analytics workspace show \
  --resource-group "$RG" --workspace-name "$LAW" \
  --query customerId --output tsv)

# 1. Enable the Update Management solution in the workspace
#    (REST call — no direct az cli surface for solutions)
az rest --method PUT \
  --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/<mgmt-sub-id>/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.OperationsManagement/solutions/Updates($LAW)?api-version=2015-11-01-preview" \
  --body "{
    \"location\": \"eastus\",
    \"plan\": {
      \"name\": \"Updates($LAW)\",
      \"publisher\": \"Microsoft\",
      \"product\": \"OMSGallery/Updates\",
      \"promotionCode\": \"\"
    },
    \"properties\": {
      \"workspaceResourceId\": \"/subscriptions/<mgmt-sub-id>/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$LAW\"
    }
  }"

# 2. Create a recurring schedule — every Sunday at 02:00 UTC
az automation schedule create \
  --resource-group "$RG" \
  --automation-account-name "$AA" \
  --name "$SCHEDULE" \
  --frequency Week \
  --interval 1 \
  --start-time "2024-01-07T02:00:00+00:00" \
  --time-zone "UTC" \
  --week-days Sunday \
  --description "Weekly patching window — Sundays 02:00 UTC"

# 3. Query missing patches via Log Analytics (KQL)
az monitor log-analytics query \
  --workspace "$LAW_WORKSPACE_ID" \
  --analytics-query "
    Update
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
    | where UpdateState == 'Needed' and Optional == false
    | summarize MissingCount=count() by Computer, Classification
    | sort by MissingCount desc
  " \
  --timespan P1D \
  --output table

echo "Update Management configured."
Enabling Microsoft Defender for Cloud

Defender for Cloud provides cloud security posture management (CSPM) and workload protection (CWP) across all subscriptions. The management subscription hosts the central Log Analytics workspace that Defender uses as its data store. Each plan is enabled per-subscription; in ESLZ this is handled by Azure Policy at the alz-landing-zones management group scope (DeployIfNotExists effect) so every new landing zone subscription automatically gets Defender plans enabled. The management subscription itself also needs plans enabled since it hosts the workspace and Automation Account.

# Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud plans in the management subscription
# and configure the central Log Analytics workspace as the data destination.

SUB_MGMT='<management-sub-id>'
LOG_WS_ID='/subscriptions/<management-sub-id>/resourceGroups/rg-management-prod-eastus/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/log-management-prod-eastus'

# Set the default Log Analytics workspace for Defender for Cloud
az security workspace-setting create \
  --name default \
  --target-workspace $LOG_WS_ID \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Enable Defender CSPM (Cloud Security Posture Management) — provides secure score
az security pricing create \
  --name CloudPosture \
  --tier Standard \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Enable Defender for Servers Plan 2 (includes Defender for Endpoint integration)
az security pricing create \
  --name VirtualMachines \
  --tier Standard \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Enable Defender for SQL on Azure VMs
az security pricing create \
  --name SqlServerVirtualMachines \
  --tier Standard \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Enable Defender for Containers
az security pricing create \
  --name Containers \
  --tier Standard \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Enable Defender for Storage
az security pricing create \
  --name StorageAccounts \
  --tier Standard \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT

# Verify all Defender plans are enabled
az security pricing list \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT \
  --query '[].{Name:name, Tier:pricingTier}' \
  -o table

# Export Defender security alerts to the Log Analytics workspace
az security automation create \
  --name 'export-alerts-to-log' \
  --resource-group rg-management-prod-eastus \
  --scopes '[{"description":"All subscriptions","scopePath":"/subscriptions/<management-sub-id>"}]' \
  --sources '[{"eventSource":"Alerts","ruleSets":[{"rules":[{"propertyType":"string","propertyPath":"Severity","operator":"NotEquals","expectedValue":"Low"}]}]}]' \
  --actions '[{"actionType":"Workspace","workspaceResourceId":"'$LOG_WS_ID'"}]' \
  --location eastus \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT
Routing Diagnostic Settings via Azure Policy

Every resource in every subscription should route its diagnostic logs to the central Log Analytics workspace. This is enforced via an Azure Policy with DeployIfNotExists effect assigned at the ALZ root management group. The policy requires a system-assigned identity (Monitoring Contributor) to create diagnostic settings on non-compliant resources. After assignment, a remediation task deploys the diagnostic settings retroactively to all existing resources — without the remediation, only new resources created after policy assignment are covered.

# Deploy Azure Policy to route diagnostic settings from all subscriptions
# to the central Log Analytics workspace in the management subscription.
# This policy is assigned at the ALZ root MG — it applies to all subscriptions.

ALZ_SCOPE='/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/alz'
LOG_WS_ID='/subscriptions/<management-sub-id>/resourceGroups/rg-management-prod-eastus/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/log-management-prod-eastus'

# Policy: Deploy diagnostic settings for Activity Logs to the workspace
az policy assignment create \
  --name 'diag-activity-log-to-ws' \
  --display-name 'Deploy diagnostic settings for Activity Log to Log Analytics workspace' \
  --policy '2465583e-4e78-4c2b-8ab1-759b014fcab9' \
  --scope $ALZ_SCOPE \
  --params "{\"logAnalytics\":{\"value\":\"$LOG_WS_ID\"}}" \
  --identity-type SystemAssigned \
  --location eastus

# Assign Monitoring Contributor to the policy identity (needed to create diag settings)
POLICY_IDENTITY=$(az policy assignment show \
  --name 'diag-activity-log-to-ws' \
  --scope $ALZ_SCOPE \
  --query identity.principalId -o tsv)

az role assignment create \
  --role 'Monitoring Contributor' \
  --assignee-object-id $POLICY_IDENTITY \
  --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
  --scope $ALZ_SCOPE

# Trigger remediation to apply to existing subscriptions
az policy remediation create \
  --name 'remediate-diag-activity-log' \
  --policy-assignment 'diag-activity-log-to-ws' \
  --resource-discovery-mode ExistingNonCompliant \
  --scope $ALZ_SCOPE

# Monitor remediation progress
az policy remediation show \
  --name 'remediate-diag-activity-log' \
  --scope $ALZ_SCOPE \
  --query '{Status:provisioningState, Created:createdOn, Resources:resourceCount}' \
  -o json
Enabling Microsoft Sentinel

Microsoft Sentinel is a cloud-native SIEM built on top of Log Analytics. Since the central workspace already collects Activity Logs, security events, and Defender alerts from all subscriptions, enabling Sentinel on that workspace makes all existing data queryable for threat detection and incident management without additional ingestion cost. Sentinel adds data connectors, analytics rules, workbooks, and automation playbooks (Logic Apps) on top of the raw log data.

# Enable Microsoft Sentinel in the management subscription
# Sentinel uses the central Log Analytics workspace as its data store.
# It adds SIEM/SOAR capabilities on top of the logs already flowing to the workspace.

SUB_MGMT='<management-sub-id>'
RG='rg-management-prod-eastus'
WS_NAME='log-management-prod-eastus'

# Enable Sentinel on the Log Analytics workspace
az sentinel onboarding-state create \
  --workspace-name $WS_NAME \
  --resource-group $RG \
  --subscription $SUB_MGMT \
  --sentinel-onboarding-state-name 'default'

# Enable the Azure Activity data connector (Activity Logs from all subscriptions)
az rest \
  --method PUT \
  --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB_MGMT/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$WS_NAME/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/dataConnectors/AzureActivity?api-version=2022-12-01-preview" \
  --body '{"kind":"AzureActivity","properties":{"dataTypes":{"alerts":{"state":"enabled"}},"tenantId":"<tenant-id>"}}'

# Enable the Microsoft Defender for Cloud data connector
az rest \
  --method PUT \
  --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB_MGMT/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$WS_NAME/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/dataConnectors/MicrosoftDefenderAdvancedThreatProtection?api-version=2022-12-01-preview" \
  --body '{"kind":"MicrosoftDefenderAdvancedThreatProtection","properties":{"dataTypes":{"alerts":{"state":"enabled"}},"tenantId":"<tenant-id>"}}'

# Create a basic Sentinel Analytics rule: alert on multiple failed logins
az rest \
  --method PUT \
  --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB_MGMT/resourceGroups/$RG/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/$WS_NAME/providers/Microsoft.SecurityInsights/alertRules/failed-login-rule?api-version=2022-12-01-preview" \
  --body '{
    "kind": "Scheduled",
    "properties": {
      "displayName": "Multiple failed logins from same IP",
      "enabled": true,
      "severity": "Medium",
      "query": "SigninLogs | where ResultType != 0 | summarize count() by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m) | where count_ > 10",
      "queryFrequency": "PT5M",
      "queryPeriod": "PT5M",
      "triggerOperator": "GreaterThan",
      "triggerThreshold": 0
    }
  }'

echo 'Sentinel enabled and configured.'
Management Subscription Health Queries

Five KQL queries provide a weekly health check for the management subscription: subscriptions not sending Activity Logs (indicates broken diagnostic settings), workspace ingestion volume by data type (cost monitoring), Defender alert summary (security posture), VMs with critical missing patches (patch compliance), and Automation Account job failures (runbook reliability). These form the backbone of the platform team's weekly operational report.

// KQL queries for management subscription health — run in Log Analytics workspace

// 1. Which subscriptions are NOT sending Activity Logs to the central workspace?
// (Indirect: check which subscriptions have no AzureActivity records in the last 24h)
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize LastActivity = max(TimeGenerated) by SubscriptionId
| join kind=rightouter (
    // Replace with your known subscription IDs
    datatable(SubscriptionId:string)[
      '<sub-connectivity>',
      '<sub-identity>',
      '<sub-management>',
      '<sub-payments-prod>'
    ]
) on SubscriptionId
| where isnull(LastActivity)
| project SubscriptionId, Status='No activity logs received in 24h'

// 2. Log Analytics workspace ingestion volume by subscription (last 7 days)
Usage
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where IsBillable == true
| summarize IngestionGB = sum(Quantity) / 1000 by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), DataType
| order by TimeGenerated desc, IngestionGB desc

// 3. Defender for Cloud alerts by severity (last 30 days)
SecurityAlert
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| summarize Count = count() by AlertSeverity, AlertName
| order by AlertSeverity asc, Count desc

// 4. Update compliance: VMs with critical missing patches
Update
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Classification =~ 'Critical Updates' and UpdateState =~ 'Needed'
| summarize MissingCount = count() by Computer, SubscriptionId
| order by MissingCount desc

// 5. Automation Account job failures in the last 7 days
AzureDiagnostics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResourceType =~ 'AUTOMATIONACCOUNTS'
| where ResultType =~ 'Failed'
| summarize FailCount = count() by RunbookName_s, ResultDescription_s
| order by FailCount desc
Common Management Subscription Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Fix
Creating separate Log Analytics workspaces per subscription
No cross-subscription correlation; alert rules can't span subscriptions; higher cost from multiple workspaces
One workspace per environment tier (prod/non-prod) in the management subscription; all subscriptions route to it
Not running the policy remediation task after assignment
Existing resources (created before policy assignment) never get diagnostic settings deployed
Always run az policy remediation create after assigning DeployIfNotExists policies; use ExistingNonCompliant discovery mode
Enabling Sentinel before the workspace has enough data
Analytics rules fire on empty tables producing false negatives; tuning is impossible without baseline
Enable Sentinel after at least 7 days of log ingestion from all data connectors
Leaving Defender plans on Free tier in production
No threat detection, no secure score, no EDR integration; only basic recommendations
Enable Standard tier for CSPM at minimum; enable Defender for Servers P2 for all VM subscriptions
Storing runbook credentials in Automation Account variables (plaintext)
Any user with Reader on the Automation Account can read the credential
Use Automation Account credentials (encrypted) or Key Vault references in runbooks; never use string variables for secrets
Not setting Log Analytics retention beyond 30 days
Security investigations requiring more than 30 days of data cannot access older logs
Set retention to 90 days (included in PerGB2018 pricing up to 31 days free; additional days billed) for compliance and incident response
Summary

The Management subscription implements the observability backbone of the ESLZ platform. A single Log Analytics workspace receives diagnostic logs from every subscription via Azure Policy-enforced diagnostic settings; the Automation Account provides agentless update and configuration management through its workspace link; and Azure Monitor alert rules backed by Action Groups ensure that platform anomalies surface to on-call engineers without manual per-subscription configuration. Together these three resources let a small platform team maintain consistent operational hygiene across a fleet of landing zones.
